steps.title: Identify the question type
steps.content: Text-completion/illustrate-the-claim question. The claim is that Marianne can persuade others of the rightness of her artistic judgments. The correct quotation must show her actively convincing someone (Willoughby) to accept her views on art.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'she gave him such a look of approbation as secured the largest share of his discourse to herself': shows Marianne approving of Willoughby's existing taste and steering conversation toward herself; it does not depict her persuading him of an artistic judgment. ✗
  • Choice B — 'if any difference appeared, any objection arose, it lasted no longer than till the force of her arguments and the brightness of her eyes could be displayed': explicitly shows Marianne overcoming Willoughby's objections through "the force of her arguments" — the very persuasion the claim describes. ✓
  • Choice C — 'she had neither shyness nor reserve': characterizes her willingness to discuss favorite topics, not her ability to win others over to her judgments. ✗
  • Choice D — 'their enjoyment...was mutual...from a general conformity of judgment': describes pre-existing agreement between the two, not Marianne persuading anyone of anything. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
